name: How to find your Photography Style: Identify and Develop it

step.name: Note composition techniques
step.text: Examining the composition of favourite photos has an influence on understanding one's preferred visual language. Photographers should pay attention to how they frame subjects, use negative space, or employ techniques like the rule of thirds . They might notice a tendency to fill the frame or a preference for minimalist composition s. Understanding these choices helps to refine and replicate successful compositional strategies in future shoots.

step.name: Try various photography styles
step.text: Photography genres serve as categories that classify the diverse world of image-making. From portraiture to photojournalism to product photography, there's a vast array of styles to explore. Each genre has its unique characteristics and techniques, encompassing composition, lighting, and post-processing methods. Photographers should learn about their options before selecting a style to focus on. Whether one's interest lies in people, animals, nature, food, or fashion, there's a type of photography geared towards that specific subject matter.

step.name: Find what resonates with you
step.text: As photographers experiment with different genres and push their boundaries, they should pay attention to what truly resonates with them . This process of self-discovery involves analysing favourite photos, identifying patterns, and understanding personal preferences. By practising consistently and mixing up their approach - sometimes shooting spontaneously, other times planning meticulously - photographers can hone their skills and develop their unique style. It's important to remember that defining one's style takes time and isn't always easy, but it's crucial for building a portfolio tha...

step.name: Use feedback to refine your style
step.text: Feedback serves as a catalyst for self-reflection, enabling photographers to evaluate their work and artistic choices critically. Through constructive criticism, they learn to analyse their photographs objectively and understand how to communicate their intended message more effectively. This process enhances their artistic vision and helps refine their unique style. Incorporating feedback into their practice allows photographers to continuously evolve and produce increasingly impactful and meaningful images. It's crucial to focus on feedback that targets techniques and editing while mainta...
